title: Match specification compiler
purpose: Match specifications are the query language of ETS. A match spec is a list of `{MatchHead, Guards, Body}` tuples that describe patterns to match, guard conditions to filter, and result transformations. The compiler converts this Erlang term representation into an executable form. OTP uses match specs extensively in `ets:select`, `ets:match`, trace patterns, and `dbg`.
task: Implement a match specification compiler that takes the term representation and produces an executable `CompiledMatchSpec` that can be evaluated against table entries.

requirements.title: Match spec parsing
requirements.spec: Parse the term representation `[{MatchHead, Guards, Body}]` into a structured `MatchSpec`.
acceptance: `MatchHead` is a tuple pattern with variables ($1, $2, ... as atoms or special terms) and literals
`'$_'` in body returns the entire matched object
`'$$'` in body returns all bound variables as a list
Guards are nested lists of guard function calls: `[{'>',  '$1', 10}]`
Test: parse `[{{'$1','$2'}, [{'>', '$1', 10}], ['$2']}]` — matches 2-tuples where element 1 > 10, returns element 2

requirements.title: Match spec evaluation
requirements.spec: Evaluate a compiled match spec against a tuple, returning the result or no-match.
acceptance: `CompiledMatchSpec::eval(tuple: Term) -> Option<Term>` tests the pattern, checks guards, produces result
Variables bind to corresponding tuple positions
Guards are evaluated with bound variables
Body constructs the result term from bound variables and literals
Test: same spec against `{-1, hello}` → no match (guard fails)

requirements.title: ets:match_spec_compile/1 and ets:match_spec_run/2
requirements.spec: Register BIFs for explicit match spec compilation and execution (used by OTP tracing).
acceptance: `ets:match_spec_compile(Spec)` compiles a match spec term, returns a compiled reference
`ets:match_spec_run(List, CompiledSpec)` runs the spec against each element, returns matching results
Test: compile + run against a list of tuples produces correct matches

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the full match spec guard BIF set — start with comparison guards (==, /=, <, >, =<, >=) and type tests (is_atom, is_integer, is_binary, is_tuple, is_list)
SHALL NOT implement trace match specs — only ETS match specs
SHALL NOT implement ets:match_spec_compile/1 as a BIF in this brief — the compiler is internal infrastructure
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
